---
title: "Ingest spatial data"
description: "From spatial platform output to a spatial H5MU with coordinates."
categories: [openpipeline_spatial]
order: 15
---

[`openpipeline_spatial`]{.pkg-tag .pkg-openpipeline_spatial}<span class="swatch openpipeline_spatial"></span>

Spatial ingestion lives in **`openpipeline_spatial`** (which pins core
`openpipeline`). It turns the output of a spatial platform into an `.h5mu` that
keeps each spot's or cell's location in `.obsm["spatial"]`, ready for a
[spatial QC report](qc-report-spatial.qmd) and
[spatial sample processing](process-spatial.qmd).

## Options

One entry per technology. Sequencing-based Visium can be mapped from FASTQs;
imaging-based platforms are converted from the vendor's output:

| Workflow / technology | Use when |
|---|---|
| [`ingestion/spaceranger_mapping`](../reference/openpipeline_spatial/v0.5.0/workflows/ingestion/spaceranger_mapping.qmd) | Visium: map FASTQs with Space Ranger |
| [`convert/from_spaceranger_to_h5mu`](../reference/openpipeline_spatial/v0.5.0/convert/from_spaceranger_to_h5mu.qmd) | Visium: you already have Space Ranger output |
| [`convert/from_xenium_to_h5mu`](../reference/openpipeline_spatial/v0.5.0/convert/from_xenium_to_h5mu.qmd) | 10x Xenium |
| [`convert/from_cosmx_to_h5mu`](../reference/openpipeline_spatial/v0.5.0/convert/from_cosmx_to_h5mu.qmd) | NanoString CosMx |
| [`convert/from_cells2stats_to_h5mu`](../reference/openpipeline_spatial/v0.5.0/convert/from_cells2stats_to_h5mu.qmd) | Element AVITI (cells2stats) |
| [`convert/from_spatialdata_to_h5mu`](../reference/openpipeline_spatial/v0.5.0/convert/from_spatialdata_to_h5mu.qmd) | you already have a SpatialData object |

The [Available workflows](../workflows/index.qmd) page shows these in
pipeline context.

## Example: Visium with Space Ranger

For example, to map Visium FASTQs, `ingestion/spaceranger_mapping` takes a
`param_list` with each sample's reads, tissue image, and slide/area; the
reference and probe set are shared across samples:

```yaml
# params.yaml
param_list:
  - id: sample_A
    input: fastq/sample_A/*_R*.fastq.gz
    image: images/sample_A.tif
    slide: V10J25-015
    area: A1
gex_reference: reference_gex/
probe_set: Visium_Human_Transcriptome_Probe_Set_v2.0_GRCh38-2020-A.csv
publish_dir: results/ingestion
```

```bash
nextflow run https://packages.viash-hub.com/vsh/openpipeline_spatial.git \
  -r v0.5.0 -latest -profile docker \
  -main-script target/nextflow/workflows/ingestion/spaceranger_mapping/main.nf \
  -params-file params.yaml
```

## Key choices

- **Imaging-based** platforms (Xenium, CosMx, AVITI) start from the vendor's
  processed output, use the matching `convert/from_*` workflow.
- **Sequencing-based** Visium can be mapped from FASTQs with
  `spaceranger_mapping`, or converted from existing Space Ranger output with
  `from_spaceranger_to_h5mu`.
- **A whole cohort in one run:** pass a [`param_list`](process-many-samples.qmd)
  with one entry per sample.

## Output

Each sample becomes one spatial `.h5mu`: raw counts in `.X`, and per-spot (or
per-cell) coordinates preserved in `.obsm["spatial"]`, which every downstream
spatial step relies on. Next: [spatial QC report](qc-report-spatial.qmd).
